subject: Alaska.Ai — LinkedIn Carousel No. 14 — 2026-07-22 — The Giveaway, Surveyed. Alaska's Free Land for AI
html_body:
Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 14

2026-07-22 · 9 slides · branch claude/carousel-2026-07-22

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (8.82 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: The Giveaway, Surveyed. Alaska's Free Land for AI

2 · Paste the post copy

Alaska is moving to hand 31 square miles of public land to its own development agency for zero dollars.

The proposed conveyance covers 19,950 acres two miles north of Houston, planned as an industrial and energy district that would host data centers. The land itself is the subsidy, offered before a single tenant has to show up.

The deck walks through the deed, the data-center gap the state is chasing, the town council member fighting it, and the comment window that closes at 5 p.m. on August 19.

Is free public land a fair way to attract an industry, or should the state set a price the neighbors help decide?

3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

Automation changes this run (1)

The routine modified its own machinery this run. If a later week looks worse, this dated section is the rollback trail (each set reverts as one commit).
KindChangeRollback
fix
assets		New committed helper assets/js/akrelief.js: AK.reliefShade(cx, opts) turns a flat filled region into a shaded dimensional SOLID entirely in 2D ImageData, zero new deps. It builds a heightfield (noise.js fbm2/warp2 by default, or a height(u,v) callback for a dome/bevel/ridge), derives per-pixel surface normals from a 3x3 Sobel of the height, Lambert-shades against a single NW key (default) or the multidirectional oblique-weighted MDOW light set (azimuths 225/270/315/360 via {multidirectional:true}), maps a shadow->lit color ramp, and applies an optional silhouette mask (AK.reliefDome/reliefRect built in). This is the CPU/SVG-adjacent analogue of akthree.objectHero's rim-carve, so a LAND/object hero (the cadastral-plate / boreal-relief beat) stops reading flat. Documented in SKILL.md. Opt-in and unreferenced by any existing slide; text stays DOM/SVG and quantity is never encoded in the relief.
why: Recurring artwork-craft=7 ceiling TWO runs running (2026-07-21 beluga fell back to a flat Canvas 2D; 2026-07-22 the raised cadastral plate was a flat SVG polygon that did not read as raised even with 		revert the upgrade(2026-07-22) commit (deletes assets/js/akrelief.js and the SKILL.md entry; the pipeline reverts to no 2D form-shading helper)
pending upgrade(2026-07-22) commit (showrunner stages this set)

Aftercare (this is where reach is won)

  • Post Tuesday to Thursday, 8 to 11am Alaska time. The day signal is the robust one; you own the hour.
  • Paste the sources comment immediately, within 60 seconds of posting, so the first comment locks in.
  • Reply substantively to every comment in the first 60 to 90 minutes. This golden hour drives reach and a dead first hour rarely recovers.
  • Judge the deck against our trailing median, weighting saves over comments over shares over likes.
  • Treat the deck as evergreen. Drafts arrive daily and you choose which to post and when.
